video:video.video:title: Raves rise in Lagos as young Nigerians seek alternative party scene
video:video.video:description: For decades Nigerian clubs revolved around “table culture”— a system separating high-spending guests from others and reinforcing social status.

video:video.video:title: People staying in hotel in southeastern Beirut suburb say strike that hit it came without warning
video:video.video:description: People staying in a hotel in a southeastern suburb of Beirut said the strike that hit the second floor came without warning.

video:video.video:title: Maggie Gyllenhaal unleashes 'The Bride!' in New York
video:video.video:description: "The Bride!" stars Jessie Buckley and Christian Bale join filmmaker Maggie Gyllenhaal at the New York premiere of her re-imagining of “The Bride of Frankenstein.”

video:video.video:title: Hotel guests in southeastern Beirut suburb say strike hit without warning
video:video.video:description: People staying in a hotel in a southeastern suburb of Beirut said the Israeli strike that hit the second floor came without warning.

video:video.video:title: Hong Kong investors remain cautious amid escalation in the Middle East
video:video.video:description: Asian shares have swooned, with South Korea's benchmark plunging 10%, after a global sell-off for stocks hit Wall Street. Oil prices climbed still higher.

video:video.video:title: Democrat James Talarico says ‘there is something happening in Texas’
video:video.video:description: State Rep. James Talarico defeated Congresswoman Jasmine Crockett in a close race for the Democratic nomination for the U.S.

video:video.video:title: James Talarico wins Senate Democratic nomination
video:video.video:description: State Rep. James Talarico topped congresswoman Jasmine Crockett in an expensive and fiercely contested Texas Senate Democratic primary that once again has the party dreaming of a big upset in November.

video:video.video:title: Christian Bale is worried about Hollywood
video:video.video:description: At the premiere of his new film, "The Bride!" Christian Bale shares his thoughts about the future of Hollywood — with the potential merger of two major legacy studios and reduced theatrical releases.

video:video.video:title: Kidman on 'Scarpetta' role: 'I'm ready now'
video:video.video:description: At the New York premiere of the screen adaptation of author Patricia Cornwell's "Scarpetta" series, executive producer and star Nicole Kidman says that she was meant to play forensic pathologist Kay Scarpetta.

video:video.video:title: China to unveil priorities for coming 5 years at major annual political meeting - AP explains
video:video.video:description: China's top political advisory body opened its annual session on Wednesday with a ceremony at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories March 4
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Wednesday, March 4: More airstrikes in Iran; Iran responds with drones and missiles; Senate expected to vote on war powers resolution; Man who gave son gun convicted in connection with school shooting; Democrat James Talarico wins Texas Democratic U.S.

video:video.video:title: Paxton, headed to US Senate primary runoff, says ‘Texas is not for sale’
video:video.video:description: Longtime Sen. John Cornyn and MAGA favorite Ken Paxton are heading to a May runoff in Texas’ Republican Senate primary, setting up what's expected to be a nasty and expensive second round and a renewed push to win the endorsement of President Donald Trump.

video:video.video:title: Jamie Lee Curtis' advice for young actors
video:video.video:description: Actor and executive producer Jamie Lee Curtis reveals what she thinks young stars could learn from her at the New York launch of "Scarpetta" - based on the books by Patricia Cornwell.

video:video.video:title: Russian attack reported in Mykolaiv causing deaths and damage
video:video.video:description: The Ukrainian city of Mykolaiv was targeted in a Russian drone attack on Wednesday, two local officials said.

video:video.video:title: 'We are just getting started,' Hegseth says the US is winning against Iran 'without mercy'
video:video.video:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth says the US is winning its military operation against Iran “decisively, devastatingly and without mercy.” Both Hegseth and Air Force Gen.

video:video.video:title: AP reports from the headquarters of Israel’s rescue service
video:video.video:description: With missiles and projectiles launched towards Israel following the U.S.-Israel attacks in Iran, AP reports from the headquarters of Israel’s rescue service to show how the emergency services work.

video:video.video:title: AP explains Mojtaba Khamenei as a possible new Iran leader
video:video.video:description: Mojtaba Khamenei, a son of Iran's late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, has long been considered a contender to the post of the country's next paramount ruler — even before an Israeli strike killed his father at the start of the war last week and despite the fact he's has never been elected or

video:video.video:title: Hegseth says an American submarine sank an Iranian warship with a torpedo in international waters
video:video.video:description: The U.S. sank an Iranian warship in international waters, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Wednesday, as it intensified its bombardment with Israel of Iran's security forces and other symbols of power.

video:video.video:title: Talarico wins Texas Senate Democratic nomination
video:video.video:description: State Rep. James Talarico topped Congresswoman Jasmine Crockett in Texas' closely watched Democratic Senate primary.

video:video.video:title: Minnesota's governor and attorney general appear before House committee investigating fraud
video:video.video:description: Tim Walz and Keith Ellison were called to testify before the panel exploring fraud and misuse of government funds in the state.

video:video.video:title: Sri Lanka recovers 87 bodies from Iranian warship sunk off its coast by a US submarine
video:video.video:description: A torpedo fired by a U.S. submarine sank an Iranian warship off the coast of Sri Lanka, whose navy said Wednesday it recovered 87 bodies and rescued 32 people.

video:video.video:title: Koda celebrates International Polar Bear Day with some water fun
video:video.video:description: Koda the polar bear enjoyed some fun in the sun and got himself into a bit of pickle Friday at the Memphis Zoo.

video:video.video:title: Advice for international travelers during the war in the Middle East
video:video.video:description: The U.S. and Israel’s joint war against Iran is upending travel across the Middle East, stranding hundreds of thousands of people.

video:video.video:title: Israel orders immediate evacuations in southern Lebanon as Beirut strikes intensify - AP Explains
video:video.video:description: Israel has ordered residents of dozens of villages in southern Lebanon to evacuate as airstrikes around Beirut intensify and Hezbollah reports more attacks.

video:video.video:title: Israeli military orders evacuations in southern Lebanon and intensifies strikes around Beirut
video:video.video:description: Israel’s military ordered on Wednesday residents of dozens of border villages in southern Lebanon to evacuate “immediately” as it intensified the airstrikes on suburbs of Beirut, and Hezbollah claimed more attacks in Israel.

video:video.video:title: Hegseth says an American submarine sank an Iranian warship with a torpedo in international waters
video:video.video:description: U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth says an American submarine sank an Iranian warship with a torpedo in international waters.

video:video.video:title: 'They are toast and they know it:' Hegseth says Iran war is just beginning
video:video.video:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Wednesday the U.S. and Israel have "only just begun" their war with Iran but declared Tehran "is toast, and they know it."

video:video.video:title: Nepalis prepare for Thursday's first election after protests that ousted the previous government
video:video.video:description: Millions of Nepalis are set to vote Thursday in the first parliamentary elections since a violent youth-led uprising toppled the previous government.

video:video.video:title: Stranded French travellers make it back home amid war in the Middle East
video:video.video:description: Dozens of French travelers who were stranded in the Middle East landed in Paris on Wednesday as travel across the region remained heavily disrupted by the widening Iran war.

video:video.video:title: Elon Musk arrives for trial over claims he deflated Twitter stock ahead of purchase
video:video.video:description: Elon Musk is accused of making false and misleading statements that drove down Twitter’s stock price ahead of his purchase of the social media platform in 2022.

video:video.video:title: US-Israeli bombardment of Iran intensifies, as Tehran presses attacks across the region
video:video.video:description: Israel stepped up attacks on Iranian missile launchers and weapons factories, while Iran fired dozens of ballistic missiles at Israel and launched drone attacks across the Gulf.

video:video.video:title: UN's Secretary-General hopes 'for a swift end to the conflict' in Iran
video:video.video:description: “The Secretary-General continues to be very concerned about the situation in Lebanon where the Lebanese people are once again in the crosshairs of conflict," the spokesperson for the Secretary told the press.

video:video.video:title: Apple’s 'big week' starts with a pair of $599 devices aimed at budget buyers
video:video.video:description: Apple’s new budget-friendly iPhone 17e, a new entry-level Macbook Neo, updated iPad Air models, refreshed monitors and higher-end chipsets were all on display at a hands-on media event in New York.

video:video.video:title: Millions of people in India celebrate colorful Holi festival
video:video.video:description: Millions of people in India celebrated the colorful Holi festival on Wednesday. Thousands danced and got soaked in colored water and powder at the Swaminarayan Temple, reveling in traditional Gujarati music.

video:video.video:title: White House says Trump's 'feeling based on fact' that Iran posed an imminent threat led to strikes
video:video.video:description: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt said President Donald Trump's decision to launch Operation Epic Fury was based on the "cumulative effect" of threats and his feeling "based on fact" that Iran posed an imminent and direct threat.

video:video.video:title: What does China’s response to the Iran attack say about its foreign policy?
video:video.video:description: China said it was “highly concerned” about the Iran attack — but stayed out of the fight. Here’s what that response signals about its foreign policy.

video:video.video:title: Wall Street holds steadier as oil prices stop spiking, for now at least
video:video.video:description: The U.S. stock market is holding steadier, for now at least, following two days of punishing swings driven by worries about how high oil prices will go because of the war with Iran.

video:video.video:title: Sandra Hüller on singing Harry Styles for a 'Project Hail Mary' karaoke scene
video:video.video:description: Sandra Hüller got her daughter's approval to sing Harry Styles' "Sign of the Times" during a "Project Hail Mary" karaoke scene. (March 4)

video:video.video:title: Citing 'tremendous progress,' Trump gives the US a 15 out of 10 grade for Iran war
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump says he gives the U.S. a 15 out of 10 grade for the Iran war. The president opened his remarks, at an unrelated White House event on AI, saying that the U.S. is “doing very well on the war front, to put it mildly.”

video:video.video:title: The sea is higher than we thought and millions more are at risk, study finds
video:video.video:description: The study says climate change's rising seas may threaten tens of millions more people than scientists and government planners originally thought because of mistaken research assumptions on how high coastal waters already are.

video:video.video:title: Aftermath of airstrike that destroyed a police base in Tehran
video:video.video:description: An Israeli-U.S. airstrike which hit a Tehran police base destroyed the building and heavily damaged its surroundings.

video:video.video:title: Senators head toward vote on President Donald Trump’s decision to embark on a war against Iran
video:video.video:description: The vote is an extraordinary test in Congress for a conflict that has rapidly spread across the Middle East with no clear U.S. exit strategy.

video:video.video:title: Aerial video shows wreckage after small plane crashes in Arizona, injuring 3
video:video.video:description: A plane crashed in a Phoenix neighborhood Wednesday damaging two homes and injuring a flight instructor, student pilot and homeowner.

video:video.video:title: Putin says European leaders have only themselves to blame for their energy crisis
video:video.video:description: Russia's Vladimir Putin said on Wednesday that European governments had only themselves to blame for their energy predicament.

video:video.video:title: ZTE launches new ‘AI species’ iMoochi at Barcelona's MWC fair
video:video.video:description: Chinese electronics maker ZTE has launched a new “AI species” at Barcelona mobile fair MWC. The furry “iMoochi” is designed to be an emotional companion and uses artificial intelligence to develop an evolving personality.

video:video.video:title: Spain honors rape survivor Gisèle Pelicot with civil award for fight against sexual violence
video:video.video:description: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez awarded French rape survivor Gisèle Pelicot one of country's highest civil awards in recognition of her fight against sexual violence.

video:video.video:title: Pentagon policy chief says US and Israel have 'overlapping' objectives in Iran
video:video.video:description: U.S. Under Secretary of Defense for Policy Elbridge Colby said Wednesday that the U.S. and Israel have "overlapping" objectives for the war in Iran, with the U.S. military focusing on degrading and destroying Iran's ability to project military power.

video:video.video:title: Bodies of two girls found buried in suitcases in Cleveland
video:video.video:description: Authorities in Cleveland are working to identify two young half-sisters whose bodies were found buried inside suitcases on the city’s east side.

video:video.video:title: Behind the scenes: inside the AP newsroom during the primary election livestream
video:video.video:description: The 2026 midterm elections began in earnest Tuesday as voters headed to the polls for key primaries in Texas, North Carolina and Arkansas, setting the stage for a high-stakes battle over control of Congress and state governments this November.

video:video.video:title: Elon Musk arrives for trial over claims he deflated Twitter stock ahead of purchase
video:video.video:description: Elon Musk took the stand in a shareholder trial on Wednesday in San Francisco, where he’s accused of making false and misleading statements that drove down Twitter’s stock price before he bought the social media platform for $44 billion in 2022.

video:video.video:title: Millions left without power after major blackout hits Cuba's western region
video:video.video:description: A blackout has hit the western half of Cuba. Millions of people in Havana and beyond were without power on Wednesday in the latest outage to affect an island struggling with dwindling oil reserves and a crumbling electric grid.

video:video.video:title: Iranian drones intercepted by US cause damage and fear in Iraq
video:video.video:description: Remains of Iranian drones intercepted by the United States caused damage to buildings and sparked fear among residents in Ankawa, a suburb of Irbil in northern Iraq on Wednesday evening.

video:video.video:title: Thousands of Shiite Muslims in Kashmir protest US-Israeli strikes on Iran
video:video.video:description: Demonstrations took place Wednesday in the Indian-controlled Kashmir to protest against the U.S. and Israeli strikes and the killing of Iran’s Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories March 4
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Wednesday, March 4: Spain denies cooperation with US operations in Mideast; US submarine sinks Iranian ship; Kevin Warsh formally nominated as next Fed chair; Apple’s 'big week' launches a pair of $599 devices aimed at budget buyers.

video:video.video:title: Expect price increases on a range of goods the longer the war in the Middle East lasts
video:video.video:description: The Iran war has effectively halted oil tanker movement in the Strait of Hormuz. It's also disrupting the global supply chain, affecting everything from pharmaceuticals from India, semiconductors from Asia and oil-derived products like fertilizers

video:video.video:title: Hong Kong expat recounts search for safety
video:video.video:description: Agnes Chen Pun, a Hong Kong expatriate who moved to Dubai with her husband and their 1 1/2-year-old last year, said she has tried to keep her family safe amid escalating tensions in the United Arab Emirates.

video:video.video:title: European couple stranded with their children
video:video.video:description: Viktoriia Lokhmatova, who lives in Serbia, and Michael Crepin, who lives in Belgium, had traveled to Dubai with their children for a special milestone.

video:video.video:title: Midflight turnaround impacts tech entrepreneur
video:video.video:description: Two hours into his flight over the weekend from the Qatari capital of Doha to Barcelona, Varun Krishnan says it became clear the aircraft was no longer heading toward Spain.

video:video.video:title: Senate Republicans vote down legislation to halt Iran war
video:video.video:description: Senate Republicans voted down an effort Wednesday to halt the Trump administration's war against Iran, demonstrating early support for a conflict that has rapidly spread across the Middle East with no clear exit strategy.

video:video.video:title: US tourists stuck in Dubai
video:video.video:description: Louise Herrle, a retiree from Pittsburgh, and her husband got stranded in Dubai, where the couple had been vacationing when the war broke out.

video:video.video:title: It's Shohei Ohtani time at the World Baseball Classic
video:video.video:description: Shohei Ohtani is the show again in Japan for the World Baseball Classic. Japan is the defending champion and is expected to claim one of the two spots for the quarterfinals in the United States.

video:video.video:title: Noah Wyle says it's 'odd' being celebratory when 'the rest of the world is suffering'
video:video.video:description: Noah Wyle says it's an "odd disconnect" to be in a celebratory mood during awards season while "innocent people are suffering."

video:video.video:title: Canada offers evacuation options for citizens in the Middle East
video:video.video:description: Foreign Minister Anita Anand announced Wednesday that Canada is arranging evacuation options for Canadians wanting to leave the Middle East.

video:video.video:title: Turkey issuing 'warnings' to prevent missile threats after Iranian ballistic missile intercepted
video:video.video:description: Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan said Turkey is issuing "warnings" to prevent missile threats, after NATO defenses on Wednesday intercepted an Iranian ballistic missile heading toward its airspace.

video:video.video:title: Hong Kong expat recounts search for safety and way out of Dubai amid Iran war
video:video.video:description: Agnes Chen Pun, a Hong Kong expatriate who moved to Dubai last year, recounted her efforts to keep her family safe amid escalating tensions in the UAE.

video:video.video:title: Department of Defense video shows US torpedo attack on Iranian ship
video:video.video:description: A U.S. submarine sank an Iranian warship in the Indian Ocean, as Washington and Israel intensified their bombardment Wednesday of Iran's security forces and other symbols of power.

video:video.video:title: Sri Lanka says 32 crew members were rescued, while 87 bodies recovered from Iranian ship sunk by US
video:video.video:description: Sailors aboard the Iranian warship that was struck by a U.S.-fired torpedo off the coast of Sri Lanka were being treated in hospital on Thursday.

video:video.video:title: Punch is growing up and ready to say 'farewell' to his prized stuffed orangutan toy
video:video.video:description: A baby monkey in Japan, called Punch, who made headlines after images of him sleeping with a stuffed toy orangutan went viral, is showing signs of integrating with his fellow primates.

video:video.video:title: A new recruit joins a team of bloodhounds built for searches
video:video.video:description: The North Dakota Highway Patrol's newest recruit has floppy ears, four legs and will be a powerful tool for finding people.

video:video.video:title: Judge says Texas' Camp Mystic can stay open despite victim’s family request
video:video.video:description: A Texas judge on Wednesday ordered Camp Mystic to preserve damaged cabins but stopped short of blocking reopening plans after a family of one of the 25 girls and two counselors who died last summer sued to keep the camp closed.

video:video.video:title: Texan James Talarico becomes a fresh face of Democrats' midterm hopes after Senate primary win
video:video.video:description: James Talarico barely mentioned Donald Trump when he finally got to give his victory speech on Wednesday night as Democrats' nominee for U.S. Senate in Texas.

video:video.video:title: England soccer star Chloe Kelly celebrated with Barbie
video:video.video:description: Lioness Chloe Kelly becomes the first soccer player to have a Barbie doll made in her likeness, as part of the brand's Dream Team collection celebrating women. (March 5)

video:video.video:title: Breast cancer survivor knits prostheses in Kenya instead of costly silicone
video:video.video:description: A Kenyan woman who survived breast cancer is knitting prostheses and training others to make them in a country where silicone ones are expensive.

video:video.video:title: Chinese Premier Li Qiang delivers speech during opening session of National People's Congress
video:video.video:description: China has set an economic growth target of 4.5% to 5% for this year, a slight decrease in the face of a prolonged property slump and other headwinds and uncertainty abroad.

video:video.video:title: Travellers from Dubai reunite with family and friends as they arrive in Lisbon on commerical flight
video:video.video:description: The first flight departing from Dubai to Lisbon since the beginning of the Iran war arrived on Wednesday night carrying around 300 passengers.

video:video.video:title: AP explains key challenges for China's National People's Congress as it opens in Beijing
video:video.video:description: China's National People's Congress held their opening ceremony Thursday in Beijing. The headline target set out for the year is of 4.5% to 5% GDP growth, the slowest number in years.

video:video.video:title: Polls open in Nepal’s first election after last year's youth-led protests toppled the government
video:video.video:description: Polls opened Thursday in Nepal's first nationwide election since last year’s violent, youth-led uprising forced the government from power.

video:video.video:title: Canada and Australia leaders urge war de-escalation, but agree Iran can't get nuclear weapons
video:video.video:description: Canadian and Australian prime ministers have called for a de-escalation of the Iran war but added the Iranians must never gain a nuclear weapon.

video:video.video:title: Millions left without power after major blackout hits Cuba's western region
video:video.video:description: A blackout has left millions of people without power in Havana and the rest of western Cuba in the latest outage on an island struggling with dwindling oil reserves and a crumbling electric grid.

video:video.video:title: Stella McCartney lets horses steal the show at Paris Fashion Week
video:video.video:description: Stella McCartney staged her Fall 2026 show Monday inside a riding hall, west of Paris, marking the Lunar New Year of the Horse with what amounted to a quiet manifesto: fashion can celebrate animals rather than consume them.

video:video.video:title: Civilians in Irbil caught in crossfire of Iran war as fallen drones damage their homes
video:video.video:description: Residents of Irbil, the capital of the semi-autonomous Kurdish region in northern Iraq, are increasingly being caught in the crossfire of the war in the Middle East triggered by the attacks launched by the U.S. and Israel on Iran.

video:video.video:title: Decades of abuse by Catholic priests in Rhode Island revealed in sprawling investigation
video:video.video:description: A new report says Rhode Island Catholic clergy abused hundreds of children for decades, while church leaders worked to downplay and minimize the scandal.

video:video.video:title: Stella McCartney shows off new collection in Paris
video:video.video:description: No leather. No fur. No feathers. No compromise at Stella McCartney's Winter 2026 show - staged inside a riding hall in Paris.

video:video.video:title: Fake images spread online as conflict in Middle East widens
video:video.video:description: Fake images spread online as conflict in Middle East widens

video:video.video:title: Azerbaijan's foreign ministry accuses Iran of a drone attack
video:video.video:description: Azerbaijan’s Foreign Ministry on Thursday accused Iran of carrying out a drone attack on its exclave of Nakhchivan.

video:video.video:title: AP video shows apparent interceptions in the sky, after the sound of explosion was heard in Doha
video:video.video:description: AP video shows apparent interceptions in the sky, after the sound of an explosion was heard in Doha, Qatar's capital.

video:video.video:title: Apparent interceptions seen over Qatar's capital Doha
video:video.video:description: An explosion sounded Thursday and was followed by what appeared to be interceptions in the sky, an Associated Press journalist in Doha, Qatar's capital, witnessed.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories March 5
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Thursday, March 5: Iran and Israel launch more missiles; US submarine sinks Iranian ship; Names of last two U.S. service members killed in Kuwait released; House to hold war powers resolution vote.

video:video.video:title: GOP senator and police drag Marine veteran protesting Iran war out of Senate hearing
video:video.video:description: Montana Sen. Tim Sheehy, a Republican member of the Armed Services panel, joined police as they dragged Brian C.

video:video.video:title: Jafar Panahi plans return to Iran after Oscars | AP interview
video:video.video:description: Jafar Panahi still plans to return home after the Oscars on March 15. The Iranian filmmaker heads to the Academy Awards with two nominations for “It Was Just An Accident,” in the international film and screenplay categories.

video:video.video:title: Fire engulfs warehouse in Florida
video:video.video:description: Firefighters battle a massive warehouse fire near the Florida Turnpike in Miami-Dade County

video:video.video:title: Sky is lit up by airstrikes in eastern Tehran as evening falls
video:video.video:description: The U.S. hammered Iran for a sixth day as the conflict in the Middle East continued to escalate on Thursday.

video:video.video:title: BLACKPINK's Jisoo teases solo album
video:video.video:description: BLACKPINK’s Jisoo says the band’s collaboration with Coldplay’s Chris Martin brought “fresh” energy to their latest album.

video:video.video:title: US traveler documents getting caught in the Middle East while going to wedding in India
video:video.video:description: Explosions in the sky woke Cory McKane in Dubai on Saturday, turning a quick visit before a friend’s wedding in India into a tense scramble to escape the Middle East as the Iran war expanded.

video:video.video:title: At world's biggest tourism fair in Berlin, stands from the Middle East are deserted amid Iran war
video:video.video:description: The Iran war has cast a long shadow at the world's biggest tourism fair, the ITB, taking place this week in Berlin. (AP video shot by: Fanny Brodersen)

video:video.video:title: Israeli tanks move to Lebanon border as fighting escalates
video:video.video:description: Israeli tanks were seen being transferred to the border with Lebanon on Thursday as fighting between Israel and Hezbollah escalated amid the wider Iran war.

video:video.video:title: Oprah front row, as Chloé embraces boho
video:video.video:description: Designer Chemena Kamali unveils Chloé's Fall/Winter 2026-2027 Women's collection in Paris, before stars including Oprah Winfrey and Olivia Rodrigo.

video:video.video:title: Senators comment on Iran war powers resolution as House prepares vote
video:video.video:description: Senate Republicans voted down an effort Wednesday to halt President Donald Trump’s war against Iran.

video:video.video:title: Iowa Governor honors soldiers killed in a drone strike, says she believes in the mission in Iran
video:video.video:description: Among the six soldiers who died Sunday in Kuwait were two Iowans; Maj. Jeffrey O’Brien, 45, of Indianola, and Sgt. Declan Coady, 20, of West Des Moines.

video:video.video:title: Kristi Noem out as Homeland Security secretary
video:video.video:description: Noem’s departure caps a tumultuous tenure overseeing immigration enforcement tactics that have been met with protests and lawsuits, as well as a wave of criticism over the response to deadly Texas floods.

video:video.video:title: 'Smartphone for pets' lets furry friends call their owners
video:video.video:description: Thought it was just humans that used smartphones? Think again. At the Barcelona mobile fair MWC, a Singaporean company is showing off a "smartphone for pets" that lets furry friends call their owners.

video:video.video:title: Trump says he's replacing Homeland Security Secretary Noem with GOP Sen. Markwayne Mullin
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump says he’s picked Oklahoma Sen. Markwayne Mullin to replace Kristi Noem as Secretary of Homeland Security.

video:video.video:title: US stocks sink after oil spikes to its highest price since the summer of 2024
video:video.video:description: The Dow Jones Industrial Average rebounded slightly Thursday after briefly dropping more than 1,000 points when the price of oil spiked to its highest level since the summer of 2024.

video:video.video:title: Photos show an emotional Savannah Guthrie visiting 'Today' show set
video:video.video:description: Savannah Guthrie made an off-camera appearance at offices of NBC's “Today” show, her first time back since her mother Nancy went missing from her Arizona home.

video:video.video:title: Jeffries applauds firing of DHS chief Kristi Noem, demands policy changes
video:video.video:description: The top Democrat in the U.S House applauded the firing of DHS chief Kristi Noem, while also demanding the Trump administration make policy changes in how Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) operates.

video:video.video:title: Austin police release bodycam videos of officers locating shooting suspect
video:video.video:description: The 53 year old suspect, who was killed by police, is seen holding an AR-15 rifle on a video captured by a nearby parked vehicle.

video:video.video:title: Defense Undersecretary Colby facing questions on Capitol Hill about Iran war
video:video.video:description: Under Secretary of Defense Elbridge Colby facing questions on Capitol Hill about the U.S. involvement in Iran war and if there is an exit plan.

video:video.video:title: AP explains: How Iran war is driving fast-rising oil prices
video:video.video:description: Global energy trade is in turmoil as war around the Persian Gulf chokes off oil and natural gas shipments, causing prices to soar.

video:video.video:title: Mullin says he will get DHS 'focused on protecting the homeland'
video:video.video:description: Republican Sen. Markwayne Mullin of Oklahoma is President Donald Trump’s nominee for Homeland Security Secretary, following the firing of Kristi Noem.

video:video.video:title: Trump fires Homeland Security Secretary Noem after mounting criticism
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump on Thursday fired his embattled Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem, after mounting criticism over her leadership of the department, including the handling of the administration’s immigration crackdown and disaster response.

video:video.video:title: Governor Abbott and prominent Texas CEOs attend ceremony launching debut of NASDAQ Texas
video:video.video:description: Nasdaq Texas is structured as a dual listing venue, meaning companies can list on the Texas-based exchange while remaining part of Nasdaq’s broader national and global marketplace.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories March 5
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Thursday, March 5th: Netanyahu visits an Israeli Air Force base; Voting is peaceful in Nepal's nationwide election; Swaths of Cuba remain without power as repairs continue; Princess of Wales visits Indian dance company in Leicester

video:video.video:title: Decades of abuse by Catholic priests in Rhode Island revealed in investigation
video:video.video:description: Rhode Island priests preyed on hundreds of children for decades and were protected by bishops more concerned with the church’s reputation than the victims, according to a new report on clergy sexual abuse released by Rhode Island Attorney General Peter Neronha.

video:video.video:title: Azerbaijan's foreign ministry accuses Iran of a drone attack
video:video.video:description: Azerbaijan’s Foreign Ministry on Thursday accused Iran of carrying out a drone attack on its exclave of Nakhchivan. (Produced by Elaine Carroll)

video:video.video:title: Britney Spears arrested, though charge is not clear
video:video.video:description: Britney Spears was arrested Wednesday night in Southern California and booked early the following morning, though the charge was not clear, according to the Ventura County Sheriff’s office website.

video:video.video:title: Travelers on evacuation flights from the Middle East land in major cities across Europe
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of thousands of travelers found themselves stranded in the Middle East after Israel and the United States attacked Iran on Saturday and Iran struck back on Gulf states and Israel.

video:video.video:title: Lebanese families displaced by Israeli attacks seek shelter on streets of Beirut
video:video.video:description: Dozens of displaced families were seeking shelter in Beirut on Thursday, with many forced to sleep on the streets with their belongings. it comes as Israel ordered residents of the city's southern suburbs to evacuate and follows days of Israeli strikes in the area.

video:video.video:title: Israelis living near border face renewed Israel–Hezbollah clashes
video:video.video:description: Lebanon was dragged into the war in the Middle East early Monday, which erupted following U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iran.

video:video.video:title: Hezbollah leader vows to keep fighting Israel
video:video.video:description: In his first public address since joining the war, Hezbollah leader Naim Kassem said the Lebanese militant group rejects the government’s plan to disarm its fighters.

video:video.video:title: Chloé shows '70s boho reverie
video:video.video:description: Designer Chemena Kamali unveils Chloé's Fall/Winter 2026-2027 Women's collection in Paris, before stars including Oprah Winfrey and Olivia Rodrigo. (Mar. 5)

video:video.video:title: Tourists’ sweat creates a salty film coating Michelangelo’s ’The Last Judgment’
video:video.video:description: Michelangelo’s “The Last Judgement” is getting a facial, with restorers removing a chalky white film of salt that has accumulated over the Renaissance masterpiece since its last major renovation three decades ago.

video:video.video:title: Smoke rises after airstrikes in various parts of Iran's capital Tehran
video:video.video:description: Plumes of smoke were seen rising across various parts of Tehran on Thursday morning as U.S.-Israeli airstrikes continued to pound the Iranian capital.

video:video.video:title: Stranded travelers share firsthand accounts from the Middle East
video:video.video:description: The U.S. and Israel’s war with Iran has stranded hundreds of thousands of travelers amid flight cancellations and airspace closures.

video:video.video:title: AP explains: Iran pummeled by airstrikes as it launches new wave of attacks around the region
video:video.video:description: Azerbaijan on Thursday accused Iran of a drone attack on its territory, and it vowed to retaliate in a sign that the war in the Middle East was spreading.

video:video.video:title: Savannah Guthrie makes offscreen visit to ‘Today’ show, first since her mother went missing
video:video.video:description: Savannah Guthrie made an off-camera appearance Thursday at NBC’s “Today” show studios to thank colleagues for their support since her mother Nancy went missing from her Arizona home a month ago.

video:video.video:title: Democratic senators comment on the firing of Kristi Noem
video:video.video:description: Democrat senators on Capitol Hill react to President Donald Trump's firing of his embattled Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem. (AP video: Rick Gentilo)

video:video.video:title: Senators reacting to Noem firing and Sen. Markwayne Mullin being named her replacement
video:video.video:description: Senators on Capitol Hill reacting Noem firing after mounting criticism and Mullin being named as her replacement Trump made the announcement Thursday.

video:video.video:title: Cuba's president signed a condolence book for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei at the Iranian embassy in Havana
video:video.video:description: Cuban President Miguel Díaz-Canel visited the Iranian embassy in Havana on Thursday and signed a condolences book for the Supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei who was killed in U.S-Iranian airstrikes in the country.

video:video.video:title: House narrowly rejects Iran war powers resolution
video:video.video:description: The House narrowly rejected a war powers resolution that would halt President Donald Trump’s attack on Iran.

video:video.video:title: House narrowly rejects Iran war powers resolution in early test of Trump's strategy
video:video.video:description: The House narrowly rejected a war powers resolution that would halt President Donald Trump’s attack on Iran. The Senate defeated a similar measure on Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Apple’s new $599 iPhone, MacBook and iPad — explained
video:video.video:description: Apple has unveiled a new lineup of budget-friendly devices, including a $599 iPhone, MacBook and iPad. Here’s what to know about the new products.

video:video.video:title: Hegseth speaking at U.S. Central Command touts 'total dominance' in skies and seas
video:video.video:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth is visiting MacDill Air Force Base in Florida and held meetings with Adm. Brad Cooper, who leads U.S. Central Command.

video:video.video:title: B2K, Bow Wow’s ‘Boys 4 Life’ tour promotes the power of brotherhood
video:video.video:description: The platinum-selling R&B group B2K has reunited for the upcoming “Boys 4 Life” tour, and they’re co-headlining with fellow 2000s superstar, Bow Wow.

video:video.video:title: China's 10-passenger electric aircraft, the Matrix, hints at how big flying taxis can be
video:video.video:description: The Chinese company AutoFlight, founded in 2017, developed the Matrix, which can carry up to 10 passengers.

video:video.video:title: Decades of abuse by Catholic priests in Rhode Island revealed in investigation
video:video.video:description: Rhode Island priests preyed on hundreds of children for decades and were protected by bishops more concerned with the church’s reputation than the victims, according to a new report on clergy sexual abuse released by Rhode Island Attorney General Peter Neronha.

video:video.video:title: Joy and relief as Ukrainian prisoners of war meet families after long months in Russian captivity
video:video.video:description: Ukrainian prisoners of war were warmly greeted by their loved ones on Thursday after months of captivity, as Russia and Ukraine carried out an exchange of 200 prisoners each.

video:video.video:title: Trump says Iran's navy, air force 'is gone,' and offers 'total immunity' to Iranian forces
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said Thursday Iran’s navy and air force are “gone” and suggested Tehran’s leaders are ready to make a deal.

video:video.video:title: Top US commander in Middle East says strikes on Iranian navy have ‘intensified’
video:video.video:description: Speaking at U.S. Central Command, which oversees the Middle East, Hegseth said the U.S. is solely controlling the timeline of activities in Iran and will do so “as long as it takes to ensure the United States of America achieves” its objectives.

video:video.video:title: Suspect arrested in deaths of 3 Utah women
video:video.video:description: A man who authorities accuse of killing a southern Utah woman, stealing her vehicle and driving it to a nearby trailhead and killing two women who were hiking together before fleeing in one of their vehicles has been arrested.

video:video.video:title: Trump suggests he'll focus on Cuba next but wants to 'wait a couple of weeks'
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump suggested he and Secretary of State Marco Rubio plan to focus the administration's attention on Cuba following the war with Iran, but want to "wait a couple of weeks."

video:video.video:title: Iran has not requested talks with the US to end the war, Iran's ambassador to Egypt says
video:video.video:description: Iran has not requested talks with the United States to bring an end to the widening war, Iran’s ambassador to Egypt told AP Thursday, denying comments by US President Donald Trump that the country “wants to talk.”

video:video.video:title: Lionel Messi and Inter Miami feted by President Donald Trump at the White House for MLS Cup title
video:video.video:description: Lionel Messi and his Inter Miami teammates were honored by President Donald Trump at the White House on Thursday for winning last year's MLS Cup.

video:video.video:title: AP explains: Thousands seek to flee Lebanon's capital after Israeli order signals further strikes
video:video.video:description: Traffic was gridlocked in Lebanon's capital on Thursday as panicked residents tried to flee after Israel's military ordered people from all of Beirut's southern suburbs to evacuate, apparently signaling plans for a major bombardment of the area.

video:video.video:title: Plumes of smoke rise in the air as airstrikes rock Tehran at dawn
video:video.video:description: Intense Israeli airstrikes pounded the capital of Iran early Friday as the U.S. apparently struck an Iranian drone carrier at sea, intensifying its campaign targeting the Islamic Republic’s fleet of warships.

video:video.video:title: US Central Command shows footage of Iranian drone carrier on fire
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military said early Friday that an Iranian drone carrier was attacked and set ablaze. The message from the U.S. military’s Central Command showed black-and-white footage of the carrier ablaze after multiple strikes hit it.

video:video.video:title: ‘The Only Way I Looked Like Bill Clinton’: Seth MacFarlane explains why he used AI in ‘Ted’
video:video.video:description: At the New York premiere of the second season of "Ted," Seth MacFarlane explained why the show used AI to make him look like Bill Clinton for one of the episodes.

video:video.video:title: Azerbaijan president accuses neighbor Iran of drone attack, calls it 'heinous terrorist act'
video:video.video:description: Azerbaijan on Thursday accused Iran of a drone attack on its territory that injured four civilians, and it vowed to retaliate in a sign that the war in the Middle East was spreading.

video:video.video:title: Power has returned to some areas of Cuba, but thousands remain without electricity
video:video.video:description: Swaths of Cuba remained without power on Thursday, nearly a day after a huge blackout hit the western part of the island in the latest outage blamed on a fragile electric grid and a lack of fuel.

video:video.video:title: First Dubai flight since Iran War started lands at SFO, reuniting stranded travelers with families
video:video.video:description: The first flight from Dubai to San Francisco since the outbreak of the Iran war arrived at SFO, reuniting families and bringing home travelers stranded in the UAE.

video:video.video:title: Americans stuck in Middle East recount finding their way home with little government help
video:video.video:description: Stranded Americans across the Middle East say they’ve been left to navigate flight cancellations and confusing government guidance on their own.

video:video.video:title: Corinne Bailey Rae celebrates new children's book, 20th anniversary tour
video:video.video:description: Corinne Bailey Rae says her friendship with music inspired her new children's book, "Put Your Records On," sharing the same title as her hit song.

video:video.video:title: People love to hate changing clocks twice a year, but can't agree how to fix it
video:video.video:description: This is the weekend clocks move ahead, causing angst, lost sleep and health issues for many. At least 19 states have passed laws to let them stay in daylight saving time if the federal government allows it.

video:video.video:title: The top photos of the week by Associated Press Photojournalists
video:video.video:description: This photo gallery highlights some of the most compelling images from around the world, published by The Associated Press during the week of 27 February - 5 March, 2026

video:video.video:title: 911 calls from immigration detention facility reveal detainees in medical distress
video:video.video:description: The emergency calls from a Texas immigration detention center included repeated suicide attempts by detainees, seizures, injuries from fights and a pregnant woman in pain.

video:video.video:title: Three newborn lion cubs unveiled at German zoo
video:video.video:description: Three Asian lion cubs were unveiled at Cologne Zoo on Thursday. The cubs, born at the zoo at the end of November, were seen exploring their outdoor enclosure for the first time.
